channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Seafarers Are Being Asked to Risk a Deadly Strait
item.description: Cutting off the Persian Gulf is hitting the global economy hard.

item.title: Oil Prices Spike as Iran War Impacts Global Energy Flows
item.description: Strikes on oil infrastructure and a near-standstill in the Strait of Hormuz are taking a toll.

item.title: The Iran War Has Rapidly Expanded Beyond the Middle East
item.description: A country-by-country breakdown on how the war has spread.

item.title: Lebanon Is Inching Toward Civil War
item.description: The country is asking whether it’s time to try disarming Hezbollah for good—and risk the consequences.

item.title: Even From Their Bomb Shelters, Most Israelis Support the War on Iran
item.description: But a long campaign would be hard to sustain.

item.title: Targeting Iran’s Fragile Water Infrastructure Puts the Whole Region in Danger
item.description: Striking desalination plants risks a spiral of deadly retaliation.

item.title: Oil Markets Are Starting to Take the Iran War Seriously
item.description: Releasing strategic reserves would be a fillip, but opening the Strait of Hormuz is the key to lower prices.

item.title: Kazakhstan’s Pragmatic Foreign Policy Faces Uncertainty Amid Attacks Across the Middle East
item.description: Since the Israeli-U.S. attack on Iran on February 28, which triggered numerous regional airstrikes, Kazakhstan has scrambled to maintain positive relations across the region.

item.title: A Deal With Iran Requires an Iran that Can Make One
item.description: US President Donald Trump’s Venezuela gambit worked, to the extent that it did, because the state did not collapse. This might not be the outcome in Iran, because the Trump administration's war partners, Israel and the Kurds, have their own interests and ambitions, which do not necessarily include avoiding the fragmentation of authority.

item.title: Iran War Promises Even More Pain for Farmers
item.description: By waging war in the Middle East, Trump is set to further strain one of his key voter bases.

item.title: Gulf War 3.0: The Iran Conflict Spreads to South Asia
item.description: Host Tushar Shetty sits down with Siddhant Kishore to examine the impact of the Iran conflict on South Asia.

item.title: The Iran Crisis and the BRICS Dilemma
item.description: The growing conflict in the Persian Gulf is a test for BRICS’ model of multipolar cooperation.

item.title: No, Turkey Is Not the New Iran
item.description: Elevating Erdogan into an existential threat is dangerous and self-defeating for Israel.

item.title: Iran Threat Exposes Britain’s Shrinking Military Reach
item.description: Decades of disarmament have come to a head under the Starmer government.

item.title: China’s Big Bet on Central Asia is Paying Off
item.description: The Western caricature of Chinese “debt-trap diplomacy” ignores the realities of China’s economic presence in Central Asia. What is emerging across the region is not dependency but interdependence, as Chinese foreign direct investment finances industrialization and mutually beneficial infrastructure projects.

item.title: North Korea Is Getting Serious About Space Weapons
item.description: The new chapter in defense planning seems like a direct response to Trump’s Golden Dome.

item.title: How Britain’s Visa Ban Risks Silencing a Generation of Afghan Women
item.description: International scholarships and student visas have functioned as a narrow but vital lifeline for Afghan women.

item.title: Sisi Faces Modest Fury Over Iran War
item.description: Fallout from the U.S.-Israeli attack reveals the weakness of Egypt’s dictator.

item.title: Europe’s “Limited Responsibility” Model Must Go
item.description: In interventions at an informal European Council meeting and the Munich Security Conference, European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde recently explained exactly what Europe must do to secure its prosperity and sovereignty in the years ahead. The question now is whether European leaders will heed the call.

item.title: Kyrgyz Supreme Court Dismissed Kloop’s Appeal of ‘Extremist’ Declaration
item.description: The beleaguered outlet, and its founder, had been branded “extremist” in October 2025.

item.title: Africa Gets Duty-free Access to China – But Chinese Companies Win on Both Sides
item.description: The move could aggravate one of the continent’s most pressing economic problems: over-dependence on the export of raw and minimally-processed minerals and materials.

item.title: Purging Millions From West Bengal’s Voters List Risks Disenfranchisement
item.description: Using institutions to remove people’s names from the West Bengal electoral roll is fraught with negative consequences.

item.title: New Zealand’s Next Steps as Middle East War Escalates
item.description: Christopher Luxon’s government has sought to deepen relations with the Gulf states, but the PM is aware of his country’s limited influence.

item.title: An AI Bubble Won’t Trigger a Financial Crisis
item.description: The AI boom may be speculative, excessive, and reminiscent of earlier episodes like the dot-com crash. But given the nature of the financing and the investments being made, the risks to the financial system are minimal, and policymakers should turn their attention to the impact of AI on the real economy.

item.title: Should Australia Offer Asylum to the Iranian Women’s Football Team?
item.description: By refusing to sing the Iranian national anthem, the players were labeled “wartime traitors” back home.

item.title: After Abe: Is Japan Entering a Takaichi Era?
item.description: The era in which the LDP was Japan’s dominant party in power was widely thought to be a thing of the past – until Takaichi.

item.title: China’s Military Threats Against Taiwan Scare the World – But Not Taiwanese People
item.description: The world looks at the PLA maneuvers and thinks of World War 3. Taiwanese, for the most part, think: “same old, same old.”

item.title: Balendra Shah’s Landslide Electoral Victory Reshapes Nepali Politics
item.description: Whether the RSP can deliver on its many promises remains to be seen. But one thing is already clear: The March 5 election has fundamentally changed Nepali politics.

item.title: Can the US Afford Trump’s Iran War?
item.description: With the outlook for US employment and inflation already becoming increasingly grim, President Donald Trump's war of choice against Iran is bound to weaken public finances and the broader economy further, creating yet another reason for international investors to think twice about buying US debt. What ever happened to America First?

item.title: China Is Learning the Lessons of Hard Power
item.description: The Iran strikes will convince Beijing to double down on military growth.

item.title: The Myth of AI Sovereignty
item.description: Even superpowers will find it is impossible to own the entire supply chain.

item.title: The Myth of AI Sovereignty
item.description: Even superpowers will find it is impossible to own the entire supply chain.

item.title: Hungary and the Future of the EU
item.description: The European Union’s central challenge is to defend its members against external aggression, whether from the US or Russia, and Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán’s regime has long frustrated this effort. Even if Orbán is defeated in this year's election, EU leaders must take the right lessons from his 16 years of illiberal rule.

item.title: Keeping Kyiv Warm Under Fire
item.description: As Russian strikes batter the grid, a corruption scandal deepens Ukraine’s winter crisis.

item.title: David Halberstam’s Warning Is More Urgent Than Ever
item.description: ‘The Best and the Brightest’ chronicles the mistakes presidents make when sending Americans to war.

item.title: Iran’s Fair-Weather Friends
item.description: Why Shiite militias in Iraq are mostly staying on the sidelines.

item.title: Why Escalation Favors Iran
item.description: America and Israel may have bitten off more than they can chew.

item.title: The Postliberal Superpower
item.description: What abandoning democratic allies will cost America.

item.title: All in the Family: Citizenship, Crime, and Plausible Deniability in Cambodia
item.description: From Hun Sen’s watch to Hun To’s boardroom ties, new naturalization records complicate Hun Manet’s narrative of ignorance.
